channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Daily Superquiz: Test Your Knowledge with the Latest Questions
item.description: The 'Superquiz' for Wednesday, April 8, 2026, is now available for readers to challenge their general knowledge. The quiz consists of a series of questions designed to test your awareness of recent ev...

item.title: European Sleeper: Overnight Train from Amsterdam to Berlin Offers Comfort and Convenience
item.description: The European Sleeper service provides an overnight train experience between Amsterdam and Berlin, covering 642 kilometres in around seven and a half hours. Departing three times a week at 10:34pm and ...

item.title: Ethekwini Municipality Responds to Severe Flooding Across Durban
item.description: The eThekwini Municipality is working tirelessly following severe rainfall over the past weekend that caused widespread flooding in Durban. Disaster management teams, alongside recreation and parks pe...

item.title: CCM recommends revival of fuel subsidy fund to ease fuel price pressure
item.description: In Dodoma on April 7, 2026, Tanzania's ruling party, Chama Cha Mapinduzi (CCM), appealed to the government to establish a fuel subsidy plan that would protect citizens from the ongoing surge in global...

item.title: Kachikwu-Led ADC Faction Plans National Convention, Appoints Interim Leaders Amid Party Crisis
item.description: One faction of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) wey dey led by Dumebi Kachikwu, wey be their 2023 presidential candidate, don announce say dem go hold national convention and appoint interim lead...

item.title: South Africa welcomes new HIV prevention medicine with arrival of 38,000 doses of Lenacapavir
item.description: South Africa has taken a significant step forward in the fight against HIV, receiving its first batch of Lenacapavir, a new HIV prevention medicine. The batch, which consists of 37,920 doses, was deli...

item.title: Dickson Talks as NDC Welcomes Politicians Leaving Troubled Parties
item.description: Former Bayelsa State governor Seriake Dickson don talk say di Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) dey ready to receive politicians wey don tire for di internal wahala wey dey scatter other political par...

item.title: NARD Declares Indefinite Nationwide Strike Over Unpaid Allowances, Threatening Healthcare Crisis
item.description: Ahahn! Big problem for Nigeria again! Resident doctors, NARD, don announce say dem dey start an indefinite nationwide strike from Tuesday, April 7th. This one come because the Federal Government repor...

item.title: Flight Diversion to Newfoundland Sparks Humour Amidst Misleading Headlines
item.description: A British Airways flight diverted to St. John's, Newfoundland, due to a medical emergency, became the subject of international amusement after media outlets described the region as a 'freezing Canadia...

item.title: The Complex Effects of Alcohol on Mood and the Body
item.description: Alcohol is a uniquely versatile substance that can affect the brain and body in multiple ways, often simultaneously. From enhancing confidence and sociability to inducing anxiety and depression, its e...

item.title: A Guide to the Best Video Doorbells for 2026: Top Picks Reviewed
item.description: Video doorbells offer an added layer of security and convenience to homes, enabling users to monitor visitors remotely through their smartphones. Among the top models for 2026 are devices like the Euf...

item.title: Centre Asks Supreme Court To Review Decriminalisation Of Adultery Law
item.description: The Central Government of India has requested the Supreme Court to reconsider its 2018 judgment that decriminalised adultery, arguing that the ruling should not be treated as binding in all contexts. ...

item.title: Kenyan brothers Chris and Ben Juma shine at Kenya Open Boxing Championships
item.description: Brothers Chris Juma, 18, and Benedict 'Ben' Juma, 20, from Mwiki, Nairobi, are making waves in Kenyan boxing after their standout performances at the Kenya Open Boxing Championships held at Kaloleni S...

item.title: ODM backs Wandayi and Kinyanjui amid Sh4.8 billion fuel probe
item.description: The Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) has come out strongly to defend two Cabinet Secretaries—Minority Leader Opiyo Wandayi and Transport CS Jamleck Kinyanjui—following accusations linked to a Sh4.8 bi...

item.title: Superlotto 6/49 and Lotto 6/42 announce winners in April 7 draws
item.description: On April 7, 2026, the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that a lone bettor from Nueva Ecija won the Superlotto 6/49 jackpot worth P171,028,544.44 by correctly picking the numbers ...

item.title: Young Royals Albert Windsor, Louis Spencer and others Enter Public Eye
item.description: A new generation of young royals is stepping into the spotlight, captivating the public with their charm and potential. This article introduces three prominent figures: Albert Windsor, crowned the lea...

item.title: How Eating Eggs Regularly Can Affect Your Health
item.description: The article explains that eggs are nutrient-dense foods that can fit into a healthy diet when eaten in moderation, typically around seven eggs per week. It notes that eggs provide high-quality protein...

item.title: Mark Carroll questions Shane Flanagan’s ability to rebuild struggling St George Illawarra Dragons
item.description: Mark ‘Spudd’ Carroll discusses the dire state of the St George Illawarra Dragons, who have now suffered nine straight losses stretching back to last season. Once one of the NRL’s most respected sides,...

item.title: Labour's Benefit Reform Plans Spark Tension Within Party
item.description: Bridget Phillipson, a key ally of Sir Keir Starmer, has indicated that further welfare reforms are on the horizon, potentially setting the stage for another clash with Labour MPs. This follows last ye...

item.title: PH Army, with US, Japan, and Australia, conducts joint Salaknib 2026 military drills
item.description: Over 7,000 troops from the Philippine Army, United States Army, Japan Ground Self-Defense Force, and the Australian Army have begun participating in the 2026 edition of Exercise SALAKNIB Phase I at Fo...
